summary: First-pass full-stack testing of The Tani Journal. Backend: 21/21 pytest tests pass (auth register/login/me/logout/emergent-session, presence heartbeat & status, posts CRUD with visibility & ownership, comments, reports, profile, list filters/pagination). Frontend: Playwright covered landing, feed, demo email login, dashboard, /editor publish via Quill, post render, theme toggle (dark class applied & persisted), online presence dots (3 blue dots rendered), delete with confirm, feed search filter (debounced), logout, mobile hamburger menu drawer. No critical issues found.

minor.issue: When both session_token cookie and Authorization: Bearer header are present, the cookie takes priority. This is documented in _resolve_token but worth noting — a stale browser cookie can override an explicit Bearer header. Not a functional bug, just a footgun for API clients.

issues: Light/dark themes render cleanly. Typography (serif headings) is consistent. Spacing & contrast look good in both modes.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 615-line module. Still readable, but if more features (notifications, drafts, drive) are added, split into routers/auth.py, routers/posts.py, routers/comments.py, services/presence.py.
@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI; migrate to lifespan context manager.

presence_status endpoint is unauthenticated and accepts arbitrary user_ids — fine for online dots but exposes whether a user_id exists (boolean map only includes known ids). Low risk but worth noting.
Cookie precedence over Bearer header in _resolve_token: if a stale cookie remains, it silently overrides the Authorization header. Consider header-first or symmetric behaviour.

Comments list endpoint returns [] for private posts even to the author — this is correct for non-authors but the author also can't fetch their own private-post comments. Currently comments cannot be added to private posts anyway (403), so this is consistent.
Post excerpt is generated at serialize time on every read; consider persisting on write to save CPU on hot feed lists.
No rate-limiting on /api/auth/login, /api/auth/register, comments, or reports — easy abuse vector for spam/brute-force. Add slowapi or similar later.
test_credentials: demo@tanijournal.com / Tani@2026 (seeded). TEST_ prefixed users created via /api/auth/register during tests.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded demo user and seeded public posts. Test-created posts/comments/reports are cleaned up inline by the test fixtures.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite at /app/backend/tests/test_tani_journal.py is the regression entry point — run with `pytest backend/tests/test_tani_journal.py -v -o addopts=''` (the repo pytest.ini auto-adds -n auto via xdist which works too; the suite is cookie-safe so parallel is fine after the cookieless-fixture refactor). Frontend was not exhaustively tested for: post edit flow via /editor/:id, comment posting from UI (only validated via API), report dialog UI (only validated via API), and profile page /u/:userId rendering. These are wired to the same APIs and likely fine but main agent can spot-check. Emergent Google OAuth (/api/auth/session with real session_id) cannot be tested headlessly — only the rejection path was validated.
